cases.description: Adult female, building collapse survivor. Walking independently with minor arm laceration and stable breathing.
Adult female found unconscious after explosion. Breathing restored after airway repositioning at 18/min.
Adult female with superficial burns on both arms. Walking and talking normally.
Child, 10 years old, with leg laceration. Walking independently and crying loudly.
Adult male complaining of severe back pain after collapse. Breathing 20/min, pulse strong, alert and oriented, unable to walk due to pain.
Child, 9 years old, trapped during collapse. No respirations and no pulse detected.
Adult female with minor cuts and bruises after train accident. Walking and assisting others.
Adult female with smoke inhalation. Breathing 20/min, pulse strong, alert, unable to walk due to dizziness.
Child around 5 years old pulled from rubble. No breathing after head tilt maneuver. No spontaneous movement.
Woman found unconscious near chemical spill. Respiratory rate 8/min, weak radial pulse, does not open eyes to voice.
Adult male limping away from collapsed building with superficial scalp wound and stable breathing.
Adult female with open femur fracture. Respiratory rate 24/min, radial pulse present, obeys commands, unable to move safely.
Adult female sitting calmly after stadium stampede. Reports mild wrist pain and can walk without difficulty.
Adult male with fractured clavicle. Sitting upright, alert, breathing 21/min, cannot lift arm but ambulates slowly.
Middle-aged woman with bruised shoulder walking steadily through triage checkpoint.
Adult male unconscious beneath debris pile. No respirations after airway repositioning.
A little boy at a carnival scraped his knee. He’s crying but walking away and otherwise seems fine.
A little girl is choking on food and passed out. Even after we tilted her head, she still isn’t breathing at all.
A little boy has eyes open and is breathing normally after a crash, but he only responds to pain and doesn’t talk when we shout at him.
Young man complaining of a bad headache after an explosion, breathing 28, but he seems dazed and answers very slowly.
Man found motionless after a bomb blast; he’s not breathing at all, even after we tilted his head.
Driver ran over a pedestrian; he’s moaning on the ground and breathing normally, but I don’t feel a pulse on his wrist.
Woman lying on the ground, bleeding badly, breathing 18, pulse present, but looking very shaken.
Girl fell off her bike, scraped knee, crying but walking to her mum.
Boy screaming in pain with a broken arm, breathing 30 exactly, pulse strong.
Elderly man lying on the road, breathing normal, eyes open, but can’t feel or move his legs.
Adult male, leg trapped under a vehicle. RR 20, radial pulse present, follows commands, but cannot walk due to entrapment and a suspected femur fracture.
Child, 3 years old. Not breathing. Peripheral pulse is present. Airway opened, still no breath. 5 rescue breaths given, after which spontaneous breathing resumes.
Child, 2 years old. Not breathing. Pulse is present. Airway opened, still no breath. 5 rescue breaths given, but patient remains apneic.
Adult male, structural collapse. Both legs trapped under a concrete slab for 4 hours. RR 22, pulse present, alert and oriented. Pain is severe.
Adult female, penetrating chest wound. RR 36, distended neck veins (JVD), and absent breath sounds on the left. Radial pulse is weak and rapid.
Adult male, entrapped under debris for 6 hours. RR 20, pulse present, mental status intact. He notes his urine was 'the color of Coca-Cola' before arrival.
Adult male, approximately 20% TBSA burns on back and legs. He is walking around the scene asking for water.
Child, 4 years old. RR 25, pulse present. Does not respond to voice; when given a painful stimulus, the child moans and exhibits non-localizing movement.
Child, 6 years old. Not breathing. Peripheral pulse is palpable. Airway repositioned, remains apneic. 5 rescue breaths given; patient begins breathing at 20 breaths/min.
Child, 3 years old. Walking toward the triage officer. Multiple abrasions and a minor scalp laceration with controlled bleeding.
Child, 4 years old. Apneic. Pulse is absent. Airway opened, remains apneic.
Adult male, amputated finger. Walking toward medics. RR 20, pulse present, alert.
Child, 5 years old. RR 24, peripheral pulse is absent. The child is conscious but very pale and cool.
Adult male, structural collapse. RR 0, pulse absent. Airway repositioned, remains apneic.
Child, 2 years old, found apneic. Palpable peripheral pulse present. Airway opened with no result. 5 rescue breaths given; patient remains apneic.
Adult male, fire victim. Significant facial burns and soot in the mouth. RR 34, radial pulse present, follows commands.
Adult male, open abdominal wound with minor evisceration. RR 22, radial pulse present, awake and oriented.
Adult male, massive trauma. Apneic and pulse-less. Airway repositioned with no response.
Adult female, non-ambulatory. RR 28, radial pulse is absent, capillary refill is 3 seconds.
Adult male, femoral fracture. RR 24, radial pulse present, alert and follows commands. Bleeding is controlled by a bystander.
Child, 5 years old. Unresponsive, no pulse, and not breathing.
Adult female, 40% TBSA burns. She is walking around the scene, crying, and following directions.
Adult male, amputated arm. Walking toward the medic with a tourniquet applied by a bystander. Alert.
Man, maybe 30s, lying still. He's breathing fast, like panting. I can't feel a pulse in his wrist. He opens his eyes when I shout.
Kid, about 10 years old. Walking around crying, has a deep cut on his arm but walking fine. No trouble breathing.
Unconscious male, struck by debris. Breathing 18 breaths/minute, strong radial pulse, cap refill 1 sec. Not responding to any stimulus.
Old man, sitting on curb. He says his leg hurts but he can't stand up. Breathing 20, radial pulse present, cap refill 1.5 sec. He knows his name and where he is.

Man, lying on stretcher (not his own). Brought in by bystanders. He's breathing 25/min, radial pulse present, cap refill 2 seconds exactly. He follows command to wiggle his toes. He cannot walk due to leg fracture.
Elderly female, not walking. Breathing 26, radial pulse absent, cap refill 4 seconds. She shakes her head when I ask if she knows her name.

Woman, sitting on the ground. She has a gash on her scalp but is alert. She says she can't stand up because she feels dizzy. Breathing 22, radial pulse present, cap refill 1 sec. She follows commands and knows what happened.
He walked out of the building himself. Has a bloody nose and a cut on his forehead but is walking straight. Breathing normally, talking full sentences.
Elderly man, found under rubble. He's not breathing after I repositioned his head. There's a faint carotid pulse, maybe 40 beats per minute.
Child, 4 years old. Runs to me crying, holding his arm. He's breathing fast because he's crying but I count 34. He has a radial pulse, skin pink. He stops crying when I talk to him.
Young man, lying still. I think he's dead because he's not breathing. I opened his airway and still no breath. No pulse.
She's on the ground, moaning. Breathing 26, radial pulse present, cap refill 2 seconds (borderline). She opens her eyes when I call her name but doesn't squeeze my hand.
He's walking, but his clothes are torn and he has glass in his hair. He says he's okay, just wants to go home. Breathing normal.
Infant, not crying, not moving. I tried to stimulate and open airway – still no breath. No brachial pulse.
Elderly woman, lying in debris. She's not moving. I see chest rise maybe 9 times a minute. No radial pulse felt. She doesn't respond to pain.

She's sitting on the ground, leaning against a wall. Breathing 14/min, radial pulse present, cap refill 1.5 sec. She follows commands to open her eyes. She says she can't stand up because she's scared.
Man, about 60, sitting on the ground. He says he can't stand up because his hip hurts. Breathing 18, radial pulse present, cap refill <2 sec. He follows commands perfectly. He's calm.
Young woman, found lying face down. I rolled her – she's not breathing. I tilted head, still no breath. No carotid pulse. She's cold.
She's lying in a corner, not moving at all. I don't see any breathing. I wait 15 seconds – no chest rise. I tilt her head, still no breath. No pulse felt anywhere.

Teenage girl, sitting on broken concrete, crying. She has a deep cut on her thigh, bleeding but not spurting. Breathing 24, radial pulse present and strong. She follows commands to apply pressure. She says she can't stand because it hurts.

Child, 4 years old, lying still. He's breathing 26/min, radial pulse present. He doesn't respond to his name but cries when I touch his arm. He doesn't follow any commands (age-appropriate: crying indicates purposeful). In START, for young children, crying to stimulus is considered 'follows commands'. So YELLOW if non-ambulatory.
She's walking but her face is pale and she's sweating profusely. She has a small puncture wound in her chest. Breathing 24, radial pulse weak. She's talking but seems anxious.
Elderly woman, lying still. I see no chest movement. I open airway – still no breath. I feel a weak carotid pulse, about 30 per minute. She's cold.
She's walking unassisted, but she has a large piece of glass sticking out of her thigh. She's not bleeding heavily. Breathing normal, talking clearly. Alert.

Infant, about 12 months. Lying still, not crying. Breathing 50/min, brachial pulse present. He whimpers when I touch his foot. Doesn't follow commands (age-appropriate). For START pediatric, RR 50 > 30 = RED.
She's lying in the corner, covered in dust. I see no breathing. I open airway – still no breath. No pulse. She's cold to touch.
Man, 20s, lying on his stomach. I roll him – he's not breathing. I open airway, still no breath. No pulse. He has a large head wound.

He's lying under a collapsed doorway. He's awake, breathing 18, radial pulse present, cap refill 1 sec. He follows commands to move his arms. He cannot move his legs. He says he's fine otherwise. Cannot walk due to entrapment.

She's sitting on the ground, holding her side. She says she was stabbed. Breathing 20, radial pulse present. She's alert and follows commands. She says she can walk but feels dizzy. She stands up and takes a few steps then sits down.

He's lying in a ditch, moaning. Breathing 8/min, shallow. Radial pulse present but very weak. He withdraws his hand when I pinch it. Withdrawal = purposeful movement = follows commands. But bradypnea with weak pulse = RED by clinical judgment.
He's walking, but he has a deep cut on his neck. Bleeding is controlled with a cloth. Breathing 20, radial pulse strong. He's alert and talking. He walks normally.
Woman, about 70, lying in rubble. I don't see breathing. I open airway – still no breath. I feel a faint carotid pulse at 40/min. She's warm.
Woman, trapped under a fallen tree. She's awake, breathing 28, radial pulse present, cap refill 2 sec. She follows commands to wave. She's calm. Cannot move due to weight on legs.
She's lying in a pool of vomit. She's not moving. I see chest rise at 24/min. Radial pulse present. She gags when I suction her mouth but doesn't follow commands. She has a strong smell of alcohol.
Elderly man, lying on a blanket. He's not breathing after airway check. No pulse. He has a do not resuscitate bracelet, but START triage doesn't consider that. BLACK.

Teenage girl, lying on a mattress. She's awake, breathing 28, radial pulse present, cap refill 2 sec. She follows commands to wiggle her toes. She says she can't walk because her back hurts. No deformity.
Woman fell from a height, breathing 39 times a minute and answering questions only partially.
Man got shocked by electricity, fell down and isn’t breathing even after we tilted his head.
Elderly woman fell on pavement, breathing fine and pulse strong, groaning in hip pain and cannot stand.
Young boy under rubble, not breathing at all even after we tilted his head.
Young man fell from a scaffold, breathing 33 breaths a minute and looks panicked.
Teenager with a bloody nose after a knock, breathing 18, pulse strong, alert and talking.
Teen boy bleeding from arm, breathing 28, but he’s disoriented and can’t answer simple questions.
Person with a small bandage on hand, walking toward the medics waving.
Woman trapped under rubble, we opened her airway and she still isn’t breathing.
Woman with a scraped knee is already walking to safety on her own.
Teen sprayed with pepper, gasping at 35 breaths a minute and can't answer me properly.
Man with a twisted ankle from a fall, breathing 18, pulse present, alert but won’t try to stand.
Adult male lying on the ground, not breathing at all even after we opened his airway.
Teen girl with a fractured leg, breathing 18, pulse fine, alert but refusing to try standing.
Toddler with a concussion, breathing 18, pulse present, but he just stares blankly and doesn’t respond to questions.
